pairs.question: Who directed All Dogs Go to Heaven 2?
pairs.answer: All Dogs Go to Heaven 2 was directed by Paul Sabella, with Larry Leker serving as co-director.

pairs.question: What studio produced All Dogs Go to Heaven 2 and who were the key producers?
pairs.answer: The film was produced by MGM/UA Family Entertainment and Metro-Goldwyn-Mayer Animation.

pairs.question: Which actors reprised their roles from the first All Dogs Go to Heaven film, and which new actors joined the cast?
pairs.answer: Dom DeLuise reprised his role from the first film. New cast members included Charlie Sheen, Ernest Borgnine, Bebe Neuwirth, Sheena Easton, Adam Wylie, and George Hearn.

pairs.question: Did Don Bluth, the director of the original All Dogs Go to Heaven, have any involvement in the sequel?
pairs.answer: No, Don Bluth had no involvement with All Dogs Go to Heaven 2.

pairs.question: How many theatrical sequels to Don Bluth-directed films did not involve Bluth himself, and which films are they?
pairs.answer: All Dogs Go to Heaven 2 is one of only two theatrical sequels to a Don Bluth-directed film that did not involve Bluth. The other is An American Tail: Fievel Goes West.

pairs.question: What was the box office performance of All Dogs Go to Heaven 2?
pairs.answer: The film grossed over $8 million worldwide.

pairs.question: Why is All Dogs Go to Heaven 2 notable in terms of MGM's animated film history?
pairs.answer: It was MGM's last theatrically released animated film until Igor (2008).
